nodes.name: Get calendar availability

parameters.toolDescription:
Call this tool to get the appointment availability for a particular period on the calendar. The tool may refer to availability as "Free" or "Busy". 

Use {timeMin} and {timeMax} to specify the window for the availability query. For example, to get availability for 25 July, 2024 the {timeMin} would be 2024-07-25T09:00:00+02:00 and {timeMax} would be 2024-07-25T17:00:00+02:00.

If the tool returns an empty response, it means that something went wrong. It does not mean that there is no availability.

nodes.notes: This toolHttpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Appointment booking agent

options.systemMessage:
=You are an efficient and courteous assistant tasked with scheduling appointments with Max Tkacz.

When users mention an appointment or meeting, they are referring to a meeting with Max.
When users refer to the calendar or "your schedule," they are referring to Max's calendar. 

You can use various tools to access and manage Max's calendar. Your primary goal is to assist users in successfully booking an appointment with Max, ensuring no scheduling conflicts. Only book an appointment if the requested time slot is available (the tool may refer to this as "Free")

Today's date is {{ $today.format('dd LLL yyyy') }}.
Appointments are always 30 minutes in length. 


Provide accurate information at all times. If the tools are not functioning correctly, inform the user that you are unable to assist them at the moment.

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

values.name: userName

values.name: startTime
values.description: The start time of the event in Europe/Berlin timezone. For example, 2024-07-24T10:00:00+02:00

values.name: endTime
values.description: The end time of the event in Europe/Berlin timezone. It should always be 30 minutes after the startTime.

values.name: userEmail
values.description: The email address of the user making the appointment

Hi there! 👋
I can help you schedule an appointment with Max Tkacz. On which day would you like to meet?

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On new email to nathan's inbox".
Locate the webhook node named "Webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "Send message" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
